title: VI
name: Civil Practice and Procedure
note: Index of Florida Statutes Title VI. 'kind' classifies each chapter: cause_of_action (a substantive civil claim you can plead), remedy (an ancillary writ/enforcement tool), writ (extraordinary writ), procedure (rules governing how to litigate), family (domestic relations). Verify every section and limitations period against current law before use; statutes are amended yearly.

key_sections.48.193: LONG-ARM jurisdiction (acts subjecting a nonresident to suit)

key_sections.51.011: summary procedure (expedited — used for eviction and where a statute invokes it)

chapters.title: Judgments
causes: Domesticate a foreign (sister-state) judgment (UEFJA)
Recognize a foreign-country money judgment
Record a judgment lien on real/personal property

key_sections.55.03: judgment interest rate (set by CFO; prejudgment per Argonaut)
key_sections.55.10: judgment becomes a lien on recording
key_sections.55.201-55.241: judgment liens on personal property (JL filings)
key_sections.55.501-55.509: Uniform Enforcement of Foreign Judgments Act

chapters.title: Final Process
causes: Execution and levy on a judgment
Proceedings supplementary to execute on hidden assets (56.29)

key_sections.56.29: PROCEEDINGS SUPPLEMENTARY (implead third parties, reach fraudulently transferred assets)

key_sections.57.105: SANCTIONS for unsupported claims/defenses + 21-day safe harbor; (7) reciprocal attorney-fee provisions
key_sections.57.115: fees against a judgment debtor concealing assets

key_sections.59.46: attorney's fees on appeal include appellate fees

chapters.title: Injunctions
causes: Injunction / restraining order (prohibitory or mandatory)
Abatement of a public/private nuisance
elements: clear legal right
irreparable harm with no adequate remedy at law
likelihood of success on the merits
balance of harms / public interest favors relief

chapters.title: Dissolution of Marriage; Support; Time-Sharing
causes: Dissolution of marriage
Child support / alimony
Parenting plan / time-sharing

chapters.title: Adoption
causes: Adoption

chapters.title: Partition of Property
causes: Partition of jointly-owned real property (by sale or in kind)
elements: plaintiff holds an undivided ownership interest as cotenant
description of the property
the names/shares of all cotenants and lienholders

key_sections.64.061: judgment; partition in kind or by sale

chapters.title: Quieting Title
causes: Quiet title / remove a cloud on title
Determine adverse claims to real or personal property
elements: plaintiff's title or interest in the property
a cloud/adverse claim (the instrument or claim challenged)
the invalidity of that adverse claim

key_sections.65.011: real or personal property; who may sue

chapters.title: Ejectment
causes: Ejectment — recover possession of real property from one wrongfully in possession (with legal title)
elements: plaintiff has legal title and right to possession
defendant is in wrongful possession
description of the land
damages for detention / mesne profits

key_sections.66.021: ejectment — pleading; recovery of possession and damages

chapters.title: Miscellaneous Proceedings
causes: Civil action for a DISHONORED CHECK — treble damages + fees (68.065)
Florida FALSE CLAIMS ACT / qui tam (68.081-68.092)
Name change (68.07)
elements_dishonored_check: written demand for payment under 68.065 served + 30 days elapsed
treble the check amount (max statutory), bank fees, costs and attorney's fees

key_sections.68.065: dishonored check — civil liability, treble damages, 30-day demand
key_sections.68.081-68.092: Florida False Claims Act (state qui tam)

chapters.title: Miscellaneous Procedural Matters
causes: Appointment of a receiver (69.011)
Deposit of disputed funds into the court registry (interpleader-adjacent)

chapters.title: Relief from Burdens on Real Property Rights
causes: Bert J. Harris Private Property Rights Protection Act claim (inordinate burden by government regulation)
Land-use dispute resolution (70.51)

key_sections.70.001: Bert Harris Act — inordinate burden; written claim + 150-day presuit
key_sections.70.51: land use — request for relief mediation

chapters.title: Reestablishment of Documents
causes: Reestablish a lost record, paper, or INSTRUMENT (e.g., a lost promissory note/deed)
elements: the document existed and its contents/terms
it has been lost or destroyed
diligent but unsuccessful search; entitlement to reestablishment

key_sections.71.011: reestablishment of papers, records, files, and instruments

chapters.title: Tax Matters
causes: Contest a tax assessment / denial of refund (72.011)

key_sections.72.011: jurisdiction to contest assessment or denial of refund; election of remedy + payment/bond

chapters.title: Eminent Domain
causes: Eminent domain / condemnation (by a condemning authority)
Inverse condemnation (by the owner against the taking authority)

chapters.title: Proceedings Supplemental to Eminent Domain
causes: Quick-take (order of taking before final judgment)

chapters.title: Attachment
causes: Prejudgment writ of ATTACHMENT (seize defendant's property to secure a debt)
elements: the debt is due (or grounds for attachment before due — removal/disposal of property to defraud)
statutory ground under 76.04
attachment bond posted
verified complaint/affidavit

key_sections.76.04: grounds for attachment on a debt actually due

chapters.title: Garnishment
causes: Writ of GARNISHMENT after judgment (reach debtor's funds/wages held by a third party)
Prejudgment garnishment (77.031, limited grounds + bond)
Continuing garnishment of salary/wages (77.0305)
elements: a money judgment (or statutory prejudgment ground)
a garnishee holding the debtor's property or owing the debtor a debt
motion + writ; 77.041 head-of-family exemption notice

chapters.title: Replevin
causes: REPLEVIN — recover specific personal property wrongfully detained (+ damages for detention)
Prejudgment replevin with/without notice (78.055/78.068)
elements: plaintiff's right to possession of the described personal property
the property is wrongfully detained by defendant
the property has not been taken for a tax/fine or under a judgment
the value of the property

key_sections.78.067: order to show cause; possession pending final judgment

chapters.title: Habeas Corpus
causes: Petition for writ of habeas corpus (unlawful detention)

chapters.title: Quo Warranto
causes: Quo warranto — challenge a person's right to hold a public office or exercise a franchise/privilege

chapters.title: Prohibition
causes: Writ of prohibition — bar a lower tribunal from acting outside its jurisdiction

chapters.title: Forcible Entry and Unlawful Detainer
causes: Unlawful detainer — recover possession from an occupant with no landlord-tenant relationship (e.g., holdover guest/ family member)
Forcible entry
elements: plaintiff's right to possession
defendant's entry/detainer without consent and without a tenancy
summary procedure (Ch. 51) applies

chapters.title: Landlord and Tenant
causes: EVICTION — nonpayment of rent (residential 83.56/83.59; nonresidential 83.20-83.232)
Recover unpaid rent / damages
Tenant claim for security deposit (83.49) or repairs (83.51 / 83.60 defenses)
elements_eviction: the statutory notice served (3-day for rent residential 83.56(3); 7/15-day for noncompliance)

key_sections.83.60: defenses; rent into the court registry

chapters.title: Enforcement of Statutory Liens
causes: Enforce/foreclose a STATUTORY LIEN (e.g., construction/mechanic's lien Ch. 713, agister, repair, warehouse, landlord's lien)
elements: a valid perfected statutory lien
the debt the lien secures remains unpaid
foreclosure/sale of the lien property; deficiency where allowed

key_sections.85.011: manner of enforcing liens (foreclosure in equity or at law)
key_sections.85.021: enforcement of liens on personal property

chapters.title: Declaratory Judgments
causes: DECLARATORY JUDGMENT — declare rights/status under a deed, will, contract, statute, or other writing
elements: a bona fide, actual, present and practical need for the declaration
a present controversy as to a state of facts
an immunity, power, privilege or right of the plaintiff dependent on the facts
an actual, adverse interest in the opposing party
the relief is not merely advisory

chapters.title: Uniform Interstate Family Support Act
causes: Establish/enforce/modify an interstate child or spousal support order (UIFSA)

key_sections.88.2011: bases for personal jurisdiction over a nonresident
key_sections.88.6011: registration of an order for enforcement
